title: Norwegian Cod Stew
description: A hearty and flavorful stew originating from Norway, featuring cod as the main ingredient. Perfect for a cold winter's night.
instructions: Heat the oil in a large Dutch oven over medium-high heat.
Add the onion, carrot, and celery, and cook until the vegetables are softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the cod fillets and cook for 2-3 minutes on each side.
Remove the cod from the pot and set aside.
Add the flour to the pot and cook for 1 minute.
Slowly add the fish stock, whisking constantly to avoid lumps.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to medium-low and simmer for 5 minutes.
Add the white wine and lemon juice to the pot, and stir to combine.
Return the cod to the pot and simmer, covered, until the fish is cooked through, about 8-10 minutes.
Add the potatoes to the pot and simmer, covered, until they are tender, about 15-20 minutes.
Season the stew with salt and pepper to taste.
Serve the stew hot, garnished with chopped fresh parsley if desired.
